plan.name: sam3-real-data-e2e

metadata.estimated_gpu_time: 60-120 minutes (9 runs: 3 variants x 3 folds x 6 epochs)

go_no_go_gates.gate: After V1: DSC >= 0.10 (otherwise abandon SAM for segmentation)
After V2: clDice improvement >= 2% over V1
After V3: V3 DSC > V2 DSC
go_no_go_gates.note: Gates are INFORMATIONAL-ONLY with pretrained=false (random init). Formal gate evaluation requires pretrained=true weights from a separate infrastructure PR.

phase.name: model-agnostic-training
phase.description: Wire build_adapter() into the training pipeline, making it model-agnostic

task.title: Add --model-family to train_monitored.py and use build_adapter()
task.description:
CRITICAL: train_monitored.py line 513 hardcodes `base_model = DynUNetAdapter(model_config)`. This must become `base_model = build_adapter(model_config)` to support SAM3 and all future models.
Changes: 1. Add `--model-family` CLI arg to parse_args() (default="dynunet") 2. In _build_configs(): read model_family from args, set BOTH ModelConfig.family AND ModelConfig.name from the model_family value (CRITICAL-03 fix) 3. In _build_configs(): extract lora_rank/lora_alpha from architecture_params and pass to ModelConfig constructor (CRITICAL-04 fix) 4. In run_fold_safe(): replace `DynUNetAdapter(model_config)` with `build_adapter(model_config)` 5. Remove `from minivess.adapters.dynunet import DynUNetAdapter` import (dead after change) 6. Import build_adapter from model_builder instead
This is the SINGLE most impactful change — makes the entire training pipeline model-agnostic.

tdd.red: (1) Test parse_args(["--model-family", "sam3_vanilla", ...]) sets args.model_family (2) Test _build_configs() with model_family="sam3_vanilla" creates ModelConfig(family=SAM3_VANILLA, name="sam3_vanilla") (3) Test _build_configs() with model_family="dynunet" creates ModelConfig(family=MONAI_DYNUNET, name="dynunet") (default) (4) Test _build_configs() with model_family="sam3_topolora" extracts lora_rank from architecture_params (5) Test _build_configs() with model_family="sam3_vanilla" does NOT create family=MONAI_DYNUNET (negative test) (6) Test source code: "DynUNetAdapter(" does NOT appear in run_fold_safe (static analysis)
tdd.green: Add --model-family arg. Use ModelFamily(args.model_family) in _build_configs(). Set name=args.model_family. Extract lora_rank/lora_alpha from arch_params. Replace DynUNetAdapter(model_config) with build_adapter(model_config) in run_fold_safe().
tdd.verify: pytest + ruff + mypy; verify DynUNet still works (regression)
tdd.convergence: all 3 gates green; DynUNet training still works

task.acceptance: DynUNet training still works (regression test). `--model-family sam3_vanilla` creates correct ModelConfig with name="sam3_vanilla". No hardcoded DynUNetAdapter in run_fold_safe(). LoRA params extracted from architecture_params to ModelConfig fields.

task.title: Propagate model_family from YAML through run_experiment.py
task.description:
run_experiment.py reads config.get("model_family", "dynunet") on line 399 for the compute profile, but NEVER passes it to train_monitored.py in either mode.
Changes for BOTH modes: 1. Refactor train_argv construction into shared helper _build_train_argv() (TDD-CRITICAL-04 fix: makes argv testable without monkey-patching) 2. _build_train_argv(): include "--model-family" from config 3. _run_losses_mode() and _run_single_condition() both use _build_train_argv() 4. Validate: model_family value must be a valid ModelFamily enum member

tdd.red: (1) Test _build_train_argv() returns list containing "--model-family" "sam3_vanilla" (2) Test _build_train_argv() returns "--model-family" "dynunet" when key absent (3) Test _build_train_argv() includes all standard flags (--compute, --loss, etc.) (4) Test _run_losses_mode uses _build_train_argv (import + call check) (5) Test invalid model_family raises ValueError
tdd.green: Extract _build_train_argv(). Add model_family to it. Wire both modes to use the shared helper.

task.acceptance: _build_train_argv() is a testable, shared helper. Both modes propagate model_family. `--dry-run` with SAM3 config shows model_family in logs.

task.title: Create SAM3 debug experiment configs and runner script
task.description:
3 separate configs (different model_family + loss per variant): - sam3_vanilla_debug.yaml: model_family=sam3_vanilla, loss=dice_ce, 3 folds, 6 epochs - sam3_topolora_debug.yaml: model_family=sam3_topolora, loss=cbdice_cldice, 3 folds, 6 epochs - sam3_hybrid_debug.yaml: model_family=sam3_hybrid, loss=cbdice_cldice, 3 folds, 6 epochs
Runner script: run_sam3_debug_experiment.py — executes all 3 sequentially, crash-resume, per-variant error isolation. Supports --dry-run.

tdd.red: (1) Test each config loads without error (2) Test vanilla: model_family=sam3_vanilla, loss=dice_ce (3) Test topolora: model_family=sam3_topolora, lora_rank=16 in architecture_params (4) Test hybrid: model_family=sam3_hybrid, filters=[32,64,128,256] (5) Test all configs: num_folds=3, max_epochs=6 (6) Test runner --dry-run validates all 3 configs without training

task.title: Fix SAM3 adapter missing methods + training concerns
task.description:
CRITICAL-01 from review: SAM3 adapters have no self.net, so base class save_checkpoint(), load_checkpoint(), export_onnx() all crash with AttributeError.
Changes: 1. Add save_checkpoint()/load_checkpoint() overrides to all 3 SAM3 adapters (save only trainable params: decoder for vanilla, LoRA+decoder for topolora, DynUNet+fusion for hybrid) 2. Add export_onnx() overrides (slice-by-slice models need special handling) 3. Add trainable_parameters() override to hybrid if missing (WARN-07) 4. SAM3-aware sw_batch_size: override to 1 for validation (WARN-02/08) 5. Guard: if model_family is SAM3 and condition is not None, log warning (WARN-04)

tdd.red: (1) Test Sam3VanillaAdapter.save_checkpoint(tmp_path) does not raise (2) Test Sam3VanillaAdapter.load_checkpoint(tmp_path) restores decoder state (3) Test Sam3TopoLoraAdapter.save_checkpoint saves LoRA + decoder weights (4) Test Sam3TopoLoraAdapter.load_checkpoint restores LoRA + decoder (5) Test Sam3HybridAdapter.trainable_parameters() returns positive int (6) Test Sam3HybridAdapter.save_checkpoint/load_checkpoint roundtrip (7) Test export_onnx() produces valid file for each variant (use_stub=True) (8) Test forward output identical before/after checkpoint roundtrip
tdd.green: Implement method overrides. Use self.state_dict() for save (captures all trainable params). Override export_onnx with 2D export + metadata for serving to handle slice loop.

task.acceptance: All 3 SAM3 adapters have working save/load/export/trainable_parameters. Checkpoint roundtrip produces identical forward output. sw_batch_size=1 for SAM3 during validation.

phase.name: code-cleanup
phase.description: Production-grade code quality: dead code removal, decoupling, DRY

task.title: Remove dead code, consolidate patterns, verify decoupling
task.description: Combined cleanup + decoupling verification (merged T5+T6 per WARN-05): 1. Dead imports: DynUNetAdapter no longer imported in train_monitored.py 2. DRY: _build_train_argv() shared helper (done in T2) 3. sam3_all_debug.yaml: remove or update (superseded by per-variant configs) 4. __init__.py: verify SAM3 exports resolve correctly 5. Prefect flow decoupling: verify no cross-flow adapter imports - data_flow: zero adapter imports - analysis_flow: zero adapter imports - dashboard_flow: zero adapter imports - deploy_flow: minimal adapter imports (ONNX export only) 6. condition_builder + SAM3: verify no invalid combinations

tdd.red: (1) Test train_monitored.py source has no "DynUNetAdapter(" (static analysis) (2) Test all SAM3 adapter imports in __init__.py resolve (3) Test data_flow.py has zero imports from minivess.adapters (4) Test analysis_flow.py has zero imports from minivess.adapters (5) Test dashboard_flow.py has zero imports from minivess.adapters
tdd.green: Remove dead code, fix coupling violations if any

task.acceptance: No dead imports; flow decoupling preserved; clean exports

phase.name: debug-training
phase.description: Run actual GPU training with all 3 SAM3 variants

task.title: Smoke test: all 3 SAM3 variants for 1 epoch
task.description:
Verify all 3 SAM3 variants train for 1 epoch without error. Catches OOM, NaN, import errors, adapter issues.
Also measure validation time per volume — if >5 min, reduce sw_overlap.
If Hybrid OOMs: reduce patch_size or confirm batch_size=1 + AMP. If any variant crashes: investigate and fix before T7.
task.acceptance: All 3 variants complete 1 epoch x 1 fold without OOM or NaN. Peak GPU memory logged. Training loss finite. Validation completes in reasonable time.

task.title: Execute full debug training (3 variants x 3 folds x 6 epochs)
task.description:
Run complete debug training. Sequential, crash-resistant. Minimum acceptable: 2/3 variants complete.
Go/no-go gates (INFORMATIONAL-ONLY with random init): G1: DSC >= 0.10, G2: clDice +2%, G3: V3 > V2
task.acceptance: 2-3 variants x 3 folds completed. 6-9 MLflow runs FINISHED. Loss decreases across epochs. No NaN. Peak GPU under 8GB.

phase.name: analysis
phase.description: Evaluate SAM3 results, tag champions, evaluate gates

task.title: Run analysis flow on SAM3 experiment results
task.description: Analysis script: comparison table (variants x 8 metrics), gate evaluation (informational), champion tagging, comparison figures. Gate evaluation function tested with synthetic metrics (CI-compatible).

tdd.red: (1) Test gate_evaluation() with synthetic metrics returns correct pass/fail (2) Test comparison table builder with mock MLflow data (3) Test output files generated: sam3_comparison.md, .tex (4) Test champion tagging logic (mock MLflow)
tdd.green: Create analysis script + gate evaluation function

task.title: MLflow model logging and serving verification
task.description: Log SAM3 champion as MLflow pyfunc. Verify load + predict roundtrip. model_family metadata preserved. 3D input → 3D output.
task.acceptance: MLflow pyfunc loads, predicts, preserves metadata.

task.title: Make deploy pipeline model-agnostic + SAM3 ONNX export
task.description:
CRITICAL-02: deploy_onnx_export.py _load_model_from_checkpoint() is hardcoded for DynUNet. Must use build_adapter() + model.load_checkpoint() with model_family stored in MLflow params.
Also: SAM3 export_onnx() overrides from T4 are now available. LoRA merge before export (R6): merge weights before tracing.

tdd.red: (1) Test model-agnostic checkpoint loading with model_family param (2) Test ONNX export valid file for SAM3 (use_stub) (3) Test onnxruntime inference on exported model (4) Test model_family retrieved from MLflow run params
tdd.green: Refactor deploy pipeline. Use build_adapter + model_family from params.

task.acceptance: Deploy pipeline model-agnostic. SAM3 ONNX exported + validated.

task.title: BentoML model import and service test
task.description: BentoML import + service test for SAM3 ONNX model.
task.acceptance: BentoML model imported. Service inference valid. bentofile.yaml generated.

task.title: MONAI Deploy MAP operator test (optional)
task.description: Verify MAP operator with SAM3. Duck-typed, no SDK needed.
task.acceptance: MAP operator compute() runs on test input.

task.title: Run trigger chain with SAM3 experiment data
task.description: Execute pipeline: data → (train skipped) → analyze → deploy → dashboard. Update trigger chain to accept configurable experiment_id.
task.acceptance: trigger_chain_results.json shows success for all non-skipped flows.

task.title: Generate paper-ready SAM3 comparison artifacts
task.description: Comparison tables (LaTeX + MD), training curves, bar charts, gate summary, DynUNet baseline comparison.
task.acceptance: Paper artifacts in outputs/paper_artifacts/. LaTeX compiles.

task.title: Self-review + PR creation
task.description: Code quality review: dead code, DRY, error handling, MLflow naming, type safety, pre-commit. Fix any issues with regression tests. Then create PR with full results.
task.acceptance: All tests pass. Pre-commit clean. PR created with results.

plan.critical_path: T1 → T2 → T6 (smoke) → T7 (full training) T7 → T8 (analysis) → T10 (ONNX) → T11 (BentoML) T13 → T14 → T15 (PR)

risk.mitigation: Merge LoRA weights before ONNX trace
